page: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is an entry-level virtual machine within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features a single shared vCPU based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC Genoa architecture running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core capabilities and overall system throughput. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is highly competitive, ranking in the top 10% with a score of 3373.08. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance, placing in the top 25%. However, due to the single shared vCPU limitation, multi-core performance is weak, and the Passmark CPU score falls into the bottom 10%. Memory performance is also constrained, with read-only bandwidth and Passmark memory scores ranking in the lower tiers, though write bandwidth remains average.

This server represents a highly cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require continuous, intensive multi-core processing. The shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity present clear tradeoffs, as evidenced by poor static web serving throughput and weak database operations. However, the inclusion of modern AMD Genoa instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and SHA-NI, provides hardware-accelerated cryptography and vector processing capabilities at a budget-friendly tier.

The Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic utility scripting, development environments, and low-traffic web serving. It is also appropriate for small-scale background tasks, simple automation, or learning environments where high memory density and multi-threaded performance are not required.
description: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is an entry-level virtual server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU on the x86_64 architecture, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While its single-core CPU performance is highly competitive, ranking in the top tier of benchmarks, its overall system throughput is limited by the single-core configuration and shared CPU allocation. Memory read bandwidth and database operations perform in the lower tiers, reflecting the server's budget-oriented design. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and SHA-NI, offering modern cryptographic and vector capabilities. This server is a cost-effective option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, development environments, basic automation scripts, and low-traffic web hosting where resource demands are minimal.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining a single high-performing AMD Genoa core with local NVMe storage for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.

2.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
